title: Does the abelian group C_7 x C_49 admit a (343,171,85)-difference set?
lean_gate_reason: The exact group row appears already ruled out in prior art (arXiv:2511.10231, 2025), so Lean formalization of the local quotient slice would be archival rather than the shortest remaining path to a sealed publication packet.
micro_paper_assessment: Frontier lane closed by rediscovery: the exact (343,171,85) row in C7 x C49 appears already ruled out in prior art, and the local eight-orbit quotient classification is only a reproducible supporting slice.
family_affinity: High: this is still a group-sensitive Paley-parameter row where C7^3 exists, but the C7 x C49 case now appears resolved in prior art.
generalization_signal: Moderate: quotient-fiber reductions and exact line-profile classification should transfer to other exponent-p versus exponent-p^2 abelian rows, but the final closure looks instance-specific.
strongest_honest_claim: The local artifact correctly derives and independently verifies a 7-term quotient obstruction with 112 oriented solutions in 8 dihedral orbits, but the title problem itself appears already ruled out in prior art.
candidate_theorem_slice: For any order-49 subgroup H, the seven coset counts of a hypothetical difference set lie, up to cyclic shift and reversal, in one of exactly eight explicit patterns.
